blocks.content: The trolley was already half full when Ethan stepped on.

He scanned the seats quickly—

then spotted her.
Of course she was there first.
Cassandra sat near the window, one hand resting lightly against the glass as she watched the city pass by like it was something worth paying attention to.

He sat across from her again.

Same as before.

Different now.
For a moment, neither of them spoke.

Not because there was nothing to say.

Because there was too much.
And neither of them wanted to say the wrong thing first.

Close enough to feel it.

Not close enough to define it.

Yet.
